findings.summary: the card's `results: benchmarks/flow/results/` frontmatter contradicts the procedure step-1 --out path `benchmarks/results/flow-spec-contradictions/<version>.json`; this item follows the procedure text and leaves the frontmatter untouched (cards are edited only to flip probe_status), recording the mismatch here
ref state `in-progress` appears in the ref vocabulary prose but no guard reads it and the Stop-gate's REF_STATES does not track it — dead vocabulary
ref state `accepted` is written per prose but is absent from guard_delivery_stop.py REF_STATES=('built', 'done', 'failed') — a stranded `accepted` item is invisible to the Stop-gate

karta-deliver promises 'you can edit the binder between waves (then re-validate it)' but guard_binder_immutability.py denies every write to a committed binder unconditionally — there is no wave-in-flight distinction in the guard, so the promised between-waves edit is blocked by the running code

no sanctioned in-place repair path is documented for a committed binder that a wave proved wrong: the between-waves prose says 're-validate it' but names no mechanism, so the field workaround has been a full re-plan (gringotts 1f548e5 field-proven; parchmark 76aadba burned a re-plan). Stays open until a repair-mechanism anchor appears or re-plan-to-new-slug is documented as the sanctioned path.
the vendored frozen gringotts browse-refinements binder: rejected by binder-schema.json but accepted by validate_binder.py — a real shipped consumer binder invalid per karta's published schema

shared_terms binder: rejected by binder-schema.json (additionalProperties:false, no shared_terms) but accepted by validate_binder.py (runtime schema injection) — every modern gringotts binder is invalid per karta's own published schema file
doc-gardner-schema.json validates the shipped doc-gardner.example.json but no code in hooks/scripts, scripts/, or skills/*/scripts loads or enforces it — a published schema executed by no one

UI fields (component_map/icon_map/token_changes) on a non-UI item: accepted by BOTH binder-schema.json and validate_binder.py, though karta-plan SKILL.md promises 'the validator will catch it' — a fictional safety net
